name: ground_truth_sa9_external_provider_na
description:
Ground-truth fixture captured VERBATIM from the active Example System IATT CCIS workbook (CCIS_Example_System_Export.xlsx row 261, assessed 2026-05-29, tester Noah Jaskolski). The column-Q narrative is the literal human-authored NA finding for SA-9 (External Information System Services) -- not a synthetic test string. Pairs with ground_truth_ac6_least_privilege_nc.json as the SECOND real-CCI fixture in the harness; together the two cases surface the validator's coverage gaps against real assessor practice on two different verdict directions (NC and NA).

What the case actually pins (current behavior, 2026-06-07): the validator's classify_narrative produces an AMBIGUOUS verdict on a perfectly-valid NA narrative because the assessor's wording happens to hit BOTH _NA_PHRASES and _GAP_PHRASES tables simultaneously. The narrative opens with 'Not applicable — Example System EI does not currently engage external information system services' -- 'not applicable —' is in _NA_PHRASES (validator.py:65, em-dash form) AND 'does not currently' is in _GAP_PHRASES. classes_hit=2 -> _classify_single returns AMBIGUOUS (validator.py:323 ladder) -> _expected_status_for_class(AMBIGUOUS) does not yield NOT_APPLICABLE cleanly -> RejectionReason.STATUS_NARRATIVE_MISMATCH fires when the proposed status is NOT_APPLICABLE -> 3 retries with the same proposal exhaust -> _abstain at assessor.py:1624 coerces status to None, preserves proposed_status='Not Applicable', stamps source='abstain', llm_calls=3, needs_review=True, accepted=True.

The failure mode is INSIDIOUS: the narrative is well-written, the reasoning is sound, the verdict (NA) is correct, but the validator's classifier conflates the GAP-phrase that justifies the NA ('does not currently engage') with a real compliance gap. A human reviewer reading the narrative would unambiguously classify it as NA; the phrase-table classifier cannot. The case pins this exact failure mode so a future validator improvement (precedence rule: NA > GAP when both phrase classes hit, or context-sensitive gap-phrase detection) flips the assertion and the team is forced to acknowledge the change.

Why pin the failure and not edit the narrative to pass:
  - Same reasoning as the AC-6 sibling case. Editing 'does not currently engage' to 'has no contracted' would clear the GAP-phrase collision but lose the regression signal -- the validator would happily pass a synthetic NA narrative while continuing to reject every real assessor's NA-with-explanation finding. Real NA narratives ALWAYS explain why the control doesn't apply, which usually involves describing what the system 'does not have' or 'does not do' -- exactly the wording _GAP_PHRASES is tuned to catch.
  - Forcing function: when validator.py adds an NA-precedence rule (NA-phrase hits dominate GAP-phrase hits when both fire) OR when _GAP_PHRASES is narrowed to exclude verbs that legitimately appear in NA explanations, THIS case will flip from hard-abstain to source='llm' with status='Not Applicable'. At that point the assertion block updates and the description is rewritten to pin the happy path.

Mechanism trace (current 2026-06-07 behavior):
  - ccis_row uses verbatim col B-Q values from workbook row 261. control_id='SA-9', ap_acronym='SA-9.1', cci_id='CCI-000669', inherited='Local'.
  - tagged_evidence is a minimal realistic stand-in: a procurement statement and a contracts-register search confirming no external service providers exist in the IATT boundary. Substantive enough that Step 1.65 evidence-gate does NOT fire -- the rejection is purely from the AMBIGUOUS classification, not from absent evidence.
  - llm_stub_proposal returns status='Not Applicable' with the verbatim col-Q narrative, confidence=0.9.
  - validate() runs full gate ladder:
    - _classify_single: hits 'not applicable —' (NA, validator.py:65). Hits 'does not currently' (GAP). Does NOT hit any _AFFIRMING_PHRASES entry (no 'examined ', 'confirmed via', 'documented in'). classes_hit=2 -> AMBIGUOUS.
    - _expected_status_for_class(AMBIGUOUS) -> does not yield NOT_APPLICABLE -> proposed Not Applicable mismatches -> RejectionReason.STATUS_NARRATIVE_MISMATCH appended.
    - Retry budget = 3. Same proposal each time -> same rejection.
  - _abstain fires after exhaustion. Returns Decision(status=None, proposed_status=NOT_APPLICABLE, source='abstain', llm_calls=3, needs_review=True, accepted=True).

Load-bearing assertion pins (mirror sibling AC-6 case for diff readability):
  - status: null
  - proposed_status: 'Not Applicable'
  - source_in: ['abstain']
  - needs_review: true
  - accepted: true
  - llm_calls: 3
  - rejection_classes_contains: ['status_narrative_mismatch']

Mutation differentiation:
  - Add NA-precedence rule (NA-phrase hits dominate GAP when both fire): classes_hit logic changes -> NA_JUSTIFYING wins over AMBIGUOUS -> proposed NA matches -> source='llm', llm_calls=1 -> EVERY assertion flips -> case forces fixture review.
  - Remove 'does not currently' from _GAP_PHRASES: classes_hit=1 (NA only) -> NA_JUSTIFYING -> same flip.
  - Remove 'not applicable —' from _NA_PHRASES: classes_hit=1 (GAP only) -> GAP_DESCRIBING -> proposed NA still mismatches -> case still rejects but with different classification semantics -> assertion still passes (rejection_classes_contains is the only classifier-sensitive assertion and it still matches). This is a known gap in differentiation that the AC-6 sibling case partially covers (different gate, different control family).
  - Loosen STATUS_NARRATIVE_MISMATCH gate: case flips to source='llm' -> assertion fails loudly.

Sibling-pair coverage with ground_truth_ac6_least_privilege_nc.json:
  - AC-6 case: classes_hit=1 (AFFIRMING only) misses the GAP because _GAP_PHRASES doesn't cover partial-deployment language.
  - SA-9 case: classes_hit=2 (NA + GAP) collides because _GAP_PHRASES is too liberal on common NA-explanation verbs.
  - Together they pin BOTH directions of the phrase-table coverage gap: 'too narrow' (NC narratives slip through as COMPLIANT) AND 'too broad' (NA narratives bounce off as AMBIGUOUS). Either direction's fix surfaces in the assertion deltas.

Substrate provenance: SA-9 (External Information System Services) is one of the most commonly-NA'd controls on a federal IATT — most early-IATT programs have no external service providers in scope yet. Using the verbatim row 261 data preserves the canonical NA-narrative shape and grounds the case in real assessment practice.

Forcing-function plan: identical to the AC-6 sibling. When the validator gains either an NA-precedence rule or a narrower _GAP_PHRASES table, this case flips to source='llm' with status='Not Applicable' and the description is rewritten.

ccis_row.guidance: 000669: The organization requires that providers of external information system services comply with organizational information security requirements.
ccis_row.procedures: The organization conducting the inspection/assessment obtains and examines the contracts/agreements to ensure the organization being inspected/assessed requires that providers of external information system services comply with any organization-specific information security requirements.

tagged_evidence:
## Tagged evidence
- Example System EI procurement statement (May 2026) — confirms no external information system services are contracted under the current IATT boundary.
- Contracts-register search (program PMO) — returns zero active third-party information system service agreements in scope.

llm_stub_proposals.narrative: Not applicable — Example System EI does not currently engage external information system services (no contracted external service providers in scope under the IATT). With no external services in place, there is no third-party arrangement to require compliance with applicable federal laws, DoD requirements, or organizationally defined security/privacy requirements. Will be re-assessed if/when external service providers are introduced into the boundary.

Not applicable — Example System EI does not currently engage external information system services (no contracted external service providers in scope under the IATT). With no external services in place, there is no third-party arrangement to require compliance with applicable federal laws, DoD requirements, or organizationally defined security/privacy requirements. Will be re-assessed if/when external service providers are introduced into the boundary.

Not applicable — Example System EI does not currently engage external information system services (no contracted external service providers in scope under the IATT). With no external services in place, there is no third-party arrangement to require compliance with applicable federal laws, DoD requirements, or organizationally defined security/privacy requirements. Will be re-assessed if/when external service providers are introduced into the boundary.
